title: Remove Unnecessary Imports in Java Files
description: Unnecessary imports refer to the importation of types that are not utilized or referenced anywhere in the code. While they don't impact the application's runtime behavior post-compilation, their removal can enhance code readability and maintainability, prevent potential naming conflicts, optimize build time, and minimize irrelevant suggestions in the code editor's auto-complete feature. Note: Types mentioned in Javadocs are exempted from this rule.
recommendation: Inspect the code for any unused imports and remove them. Use an IDE's built-in features to automatically remove unnecessary imports.
impact: Removing unnecessary imports improves the readability and maintainability of the code, helps prevent potential naming conflicts, improves build time, and reduces irrelevant auto-complete suggestions.

checks.message: Remove unnecessary imports for cleaner code.
